title: Asymmetrical bicycle pedal
abstract: The asymmetrical pedal as claimed herein is intended to replace the conventional pedals used in conventional bicycles, tricycles, quadracycles, etc. The proposed pedal is capable to produce greater torque, while the rider applies the same force on this pedal as is being applied now on conventional pedals. Since this proposed pedal is to be mounted on conventional crank arms, the leg movements of the rider remains the same as on any conventional human foot powered vehicle. So a rider can ride any foot operated vehicle mounted with the proposed asymmetrical pedal, as easily as any similar vehicle, using less effort. Or by selecting a higher gear ratio, as in the case of a conventional multi gear bicycle, the rider can ride faster while applying the same force on this pedal and pedaling at the same cadence as while riding a conventional multi gear bicycle.
claims:
Claims I Claim: 1. An asymmetrical bicycle pedal to enhance the torque of conventional bicycles, ,tricycles, quadracycles and any other human foot powered vehicle that is driven by rotary pedaling, as herein described and illustrated in the accompanying drawings, comprising of two Inverted V shaped parts which are rigidly held together; by all the other parts of this pedal to form a frame to mount all those parts of this asymmetrical pedal, namely; a front footrest which is mounted at the front end of this pedal and a back footrest which is mounted at the back end of this pedal, both these footrests being mounted on the top side of the said frame and a spindle similar to the ones used in conventional bicycle pedals and a spring loa * ded protruding rod, placed inside a tube with the tube and the spindle mounted on the downward legs of the inverted 'L' shaped frame, and the spring loaded rod placed inside the tube protruding out from the side of the pedal, that will be near the crank arm when this pedal is mounted on a crank arm, with arrangements on the said rod and tube in the form of a longitudinal groove on the rod and a screw driven through the tube with its tip reaching inside that groove to restrict outward movement of the protruding rod within desired limit when it is pushed out by a helical spring placed inside the tube, the arrangement being such that as both the pedals are repeatedly pushed down in a circular form, the rod protruding out from this pedal and the crank arm will remain separated throughout the rotary pedaling except through a distance above and below the horizontal position of the crank arm in the forward semicircle of the rotary motion of the crank arm during which period the protruding rod will get engaged 

 to the crank arm with the protruding rod positioned below the underside of the crank arm and will get both the crank arm and pedal locked and will enhance the length of the crank arm for the period they remain locked to enhance the length of the crank arm by the distance from the axis of the spindle to the front footrest, as a consequence of which the torque of this mechanism also gets enhanced through that period of engagement. 2. An asymmetrical bicycle pedal to enhance the torque of conventional bicycles, tricycles, quadracycles and any other human foot powered vehicle that is driven by rotary pedaling as claimed in claim 1, wherein the two inverted V shaped parts are replaced by a single inverted channel shaped part, with or without holes provided in the section connecting its two downward legs.
